searchProcess.robotsOrAccessNotes: dhs.lacounty.gov LA General page exists but contains no visiting-student / observership content. Keck SOM hub covers LAC+USC rotations as a placement site. USC International Physician Observership is a separate broader-USC lane.

candidateFindings.pageTitle: Visiting Student Clerkships - Keck School of Medicine of USC
candidateFindings.shortQuote: Fourth year medical students interested in doing a subinternship/clerkship in the Department of Orthopaedic Surgery spend two weeks on the trauma or hand services at Los Angeles General Medical Center.
candidateFindings.durationDecision: Max 8 weeks at Keck overall; LAC+USC = 2-week segment

candidateFindings.pageTitle: International Physician Observership – Keck Medicine of USC International Health
candidateFindings.shortQuote: Applicants must have graduated from a medical school with medical license in a foreign country. Applicants must be employed by a foreign health organization and upload a letter of recommendation from the employer or sponsor.

negativeEvidence.strongNegativeEvidence: LA General Medical Center's own page (dhs.lacounty.gov/lageneral/graduate-medical-education) has zero visiting-student or observership content. Access for visiting students is entirely via Keck SOM. The USC International Physician Observership is a broader Keck Medicine USC lane — not specifically LAC+USC.
negativeEvidence.weakNegativeEvidence: No LAC+USC-specific observership lane found. Anyone seeking a 'safety-net IMG-friendly LAC+USC observership' would be misled — the actual IMG-grad lane is via USC Keck Medicine more broadly.

nextAction: For public-safe activation: (a) LAC+USC visiting-student access is via Keck SOM (already packetized at CA/keck-hospital-of-usc.json); LAC+USC is named as a placement site for specific departments (Orthopaedics named verbatim for trauma/hand). (b) The IMG-graduate observership is a separate USC Keck Medicine lane (covered in a sub-finding here) — requires its own packet at the system level. (c) Do NOT claim LAC+USC as an open IMG-friendly safety-net for medical students; the visiting-MS access is US LCME/COCA only via Keck.
plainEnglishSummary: LAC+USC Medical Center (now called LA General Medical Center) does not run its own visiting-student or observership program. Visiting medical student access is entirely through Keck School of Medicine of USC — US LCME or COCA fourth-year only, via VSLO. LAC+USC is named as a placement site for specific Keck rotations (e.g., Orthopaedics trauma/hand). For IMG graduates, USC Keck Medicine runs a separate International Physician Observership ($300 app fee, variable tuition, 1-6 months, requires foreign medical license + foreign-health-org employment + valid US visa) — but it's broader than LAC+USC and not exclusive to that site.
driftCheck: This advanced national USCE discovery because LAC+USC was correctly classified as Keck-gated rather than overclaimed as an independent IMG-friendly safety-net; also surfaced the FIRST P101 IMG_GRAD_OBSERVERSHIP_CONFIRMED finding (USC Keck Medicine broader observership lane).
